No Poverty.reason: No direct or indirect evidence in the course syllabus links to poverty eradication.

Zero Hunger.reason: No direct or indirect evidence in the course syllabus links to hunger or food security.

Good Health and Well-being.reason: Cybersecurity skills can indirectly contribute to strengthening national capacity for managing health risks (Target 3.d) by protecting critical digital health infrastructure from cyber threats.

Quality Education.reason: The course is a Master's program, directly providing quality tertiary education (Target 4.3) and equipping students with relevant technical skills for employment in cybersecurity (Target 4.4).

Gender Equality.reason: No direct or indirect evidence in the course syllabus links to gender equality.

Clean Water and Sanitation.reason: No direct or indirect evidence in the course syllabus links to water or sanitation.

Affordable and Clean Energy.reason: No direct or indirect evidence in the course syllabus links to energy access or clean energy.

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: The course provides skills for technological upgrading (Target 8.2) and decent jobs in a high-value sector, supporting economic growth by protecting businesses and financial institutions (Target 8.10) through enhanced cybersecurity.

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: The course directly supports developing quality, reliable, and resilient infrastructure (Target 9.1) and enhancing technological capabilities (Target 9.5) by teaching how to protect systems, networks, and ICT from cyber threats.

Reduced Inequalities.reason: No direct or indirect evidence in the course syllabus links to reducing inequalities.

Sustainable Cities and Communities.reason: Cybersecurity skills learned can be applied to securing urban digital infrastructure, enhancing resilience to man-made disasters like large-scale cyberattacks, aligning with Target 11.b.

Responsible Consumption and Production.reason: No direct or indirect evidence in the course syllabus links to responsible consumption or production patterns.

Climate Action.reason: No direct or indirect evidence in the course syllabus links to climate change or climate action.

Life Below Water.reason: No direct or indirect evidence in the course syllabus links to marine ecosystems or life below water.

Life on Land.reason: No direct or indirect evidence in the course syllabus links to terrestrial ecosystems or biodiversity.

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: The course directly combats cybercrime by teaching defense against malicious users (Target 16.a) and builds capacity for effective, accountable institutions (Target 16.6) by enhancing overall cybersecurity and understanding legal aspects.

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: The course utilizes a national platform (NCHC CDX) for hands-on training, enhancing capacity in information and communications technology (Target 17.8) and fostering knowledge sharing and capacity-building (Targets 17.6, 17.9).
